text: Code reviews are an essential part of software development. When developers review each other's code, they can catch bugs early before the code reaches production. This saves time and money, as fixing problems later is much more difficult. However, code reviews offer more than just error detection. They also promote knowledge sharing among team members. Junior developers learn from more experienced colleagues, and everyone gains a better understanding of the codebase. This process helps build a culture of quality and collaboration. Ultimately, regular code reviews improve not only the code but also the entire team's skills and confidence.
